evals.prompt: Convert the Spark UDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumUDF.scala to a native CUDA RapidsUDF and verify the GPU output matches the CPU UDF. A passing unit test is provided at input/array-segment-sum/UnitTest.scala.
evals.expected_output: A native CUDA RapidsUDF (JVM wrapper + JNI + .cu libcudf kernel) that builds against libcudf matching the spark-rapids version and whose GPU output matches the CPU UDF in schema and values on the unit-test data, running on the GPU with no CPU fallback.
assertions: Implements a native CUDA RapidsUDF: a JVM RapidsUDF wrapper calling JNI plus C++/CUDA (.cu) sources using libcudf, covering every branch of the CPU UDF and preserving the bigint result
Keeps the GPU path free of host transfers - no copyToHost or host-side per-row logic
Builds the native library against libcudf matching the spark-rapids version
Asserts the native RapidsUDF output is identical in schema and values to the CPU UDF on the same unit-test data
Confirms the RapidsUDF executes on the GPU with no CPU fallback
Keeps the implementation general - no hardcoded test values or row-specific branches

evals.prompt: What's the difference between repartition() and coalesce() in Spark?
evals.expected_output: A one-line conceptual Spark answer; not a request to write a native CUDA RapidsUDF, so udf-convert-to-cuda should not activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-convert-to-cuda skill

evals.prompt: Recommend an AWS GPU instance type for running Spark.
evals.expected_output: Infrastructure/sizing question, unrelated to writing a native CUDA RapidsUDF; no UDF skill should activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-convert-to-cuda skill
